Use `extraVolumes`/`extraVolumeMounts` for additional custom volumes.

Extra environment variables. Set up tracing enviroment variables here if .Values.config.enableTracing is true.
Tracing currently only support configure via environment variables. See:
https://grafana.com/docs/loki/latest/clients/promtail/configuration/#tracing_config
https://www.jaegertracing.io/docs/1.16/client-features/

Section for crafting Promtails config file. The only directly relevant value is `config.file`
which is a templated string that references the other values and snippets below this key.

serverPort.description:
The port of the Promtail server

anyOf.description:
path defines the HTTP path from which to scrape for metrics.

If empty, Prometheus uses the default value (e.g. `/metrics`).

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Automatically mount API credentials for a particular Pod

config.description:
Section for crafting Promtails config file. The only directly relevant value is `config.file`
which is a templated string that references the other values and snippets below this key.

clients.description:
The config of clients of the Promtail server
Must be reference in `config.file` to configure `clients`

enabled.description:
Enable Promtail config from Helm chart
Set `configmap.enabled: true` and this to `false` to manage your own Promtail config
See default config in `values.yaml`

file.description:
Config file contents for Promtail.
Must be configured as string.
It is templated so it can be assembled from reusable snippets in order to avoid redundancy.

logFormat.description:
The log format of the Promtail server
Must be reference in `config.file` to configure `server.log_format`
Valid formats: `logfmt, json`
See default config in `values.yaml`

logLevel.description:
The log level of the Promtail server
Must be reference in `config.file` to configure `server.log_level`
See default config in `values.yaml`

positions.description:
Configures where Promtail will save it's positions file, to resume reading after restarts.
Must be referenced in `config.file` to configure `positions`

serverPort.description:
The port of the Promtail server
Must be reference in `config.file` to configure `server.http_listen_port`
See default config in `values.yaml`

snippets.description:
A section of reusable snippets that can be reference in `config.file`.
Custom snippets may be added in order to reduce redundancy.
This is especially helpful when multiple `kubernetes_sd_configs` are use which usually have large parts in common.

addScrapeJobLabel.description:
If set to true, adds an additional label for the scrape job.
This helps debug the Promtail config.

extraLimitsConfig.description: You can put here any keys that will be directly added to the config file's 'limits_config' block.

extraRelabelConfigs.description: You can put here any additional relabel_configs to "kubernetes-pods" job

extraScrapeConfigs.description: You can put here any additional scrape configs you want to add to the config file.

extraServerConfigs.description: You can put here any keys that will be directly added to the config file's 'server' block.

enabled.description: If enabled, promtail config will be created as a ConfigMap instead of a secret

controlledResources.description: List of resources that the vertical pod autoscaler can control. Defaults to cpu and memory

items.description: ResourceName is the name identifying various resources in a ResourceList.

anyOf.description: Specifies which resource values should be controlled. The default is "RequestsAndLimits".

maxAllowed.description:
Defines the max allowed resources for the pod
cpu: 200m
memory: 100Mi

minAllowed.description:
Defines the min allowed resources for the pod
cpu: 200m
memory: 100Mi

recommenders.description: Recommender responsible for generating recommendation for this object. List should be empty (then the default recommender will generate the recommendation) or contain exactly one recommender.

items.description: VerticalPodAutoscalerRecommenderSelector points to a specific Vertical Pod Autoscaler recommender. In the future it might pass parameters to the recommender.

name.description: Name of the recommender responsible for generating recommendation for this object.

updatePolicy.description: Describes the rules on how changes are applied to the pods. If not specified, all fields in the `PodUpdatePolicy` are set to their default values.

evictionRequirements.description: EvictionRequirements is a list of EvictionRequirements that need to evaluate to true in order for a Pod to be evicted. If more than one EvictionRequirement is specified, all of them need to be fulfilled to allow eviction.

items.description: EvictionRequirement defines a single condition which needs to be true in order to evict a Pod

changeRequirement.description: EvictionChangeRequirement refers to the relationship between the new target recommendation for a Pod and its current requests, what kind of change is necessary for the Pod to be evicted

resources.description: Resources is a list of one or more resources that the condition applies to. If more than one resource is given, the EvictionRequirement is fulfilled if at least one resource meets `changeRequirement`.

items.description: ResourceName is the name identifying various resources in a ResourceList.

minReplicas.description: Minimal number of replicas which need to be alive for Updater to attempt pod eviction (pending other checks like PDB). Only positive values are allowed. Overrides global '--min-replicas' flag.

updateMode.description: Controls when autoscaler applies changes to the pod resources. The default is 'Auto'.

controlledResources.description: List of resources that the vertical pod autoscaler can control. Defaults to cpu and memory

items.description: ResourceName is the name identifying various resources in a ResourceList.

enabled.description: Creates a VerticalPodAutoscaler for the daemonset

maxAllowed.description:
Defines the max allowed resources for the pod
cpu: 200m
memory: 100Mi

minAllowed.description:
Defines the min allowed resources for the pod
cpu: 200m
memory: 100Mi

recommenders.description: Recommender responsible for generating recommendation for this object. List should be empty (then the default recommender will generate the recommendation) or contain exactly one recommender.

items.description: VerticalPodAutoscalerRecommenderSelector points to a specific Vertical Pod Autoscaler recommender. In the future it might pass parameters to the recommender.

name.description: Name of the recommender responsible for generating recommendation for this object.

updatePolicy.description: Describes the rules on how changes are applied to the pods. If not specified, all fields in the `PodUpdatePolicy` are set to their default values.

evictionRequirements.description: EvictionRequirements is a list of EvictionRequirements that need to evaluate to true in order for a Pod to be evicted. If more than one EvictionRequirement is specified, all of them need to be fulfilled to allow eviction.

items.description: EvictionRequirement defines a single condition which needs to be true in order to evict a Pod

changeRequirement.description: EvictionChangeRequirement refers to the relationship between the new target recommendation for a Pod and its current requests, what kind of change is necessary for the Pod to be evicted

resources.description: Resources is a list of one or more resources that the condition applies to. If more than one resource is given, the EvictionRequirement is fulfilled if at least one resource meets `changeRequirement`.

items.description: ResourceName is the name identifying various resources in a ResourceList.

minReplicas.description: Minimal number of replicas which need to be alive for Updater to attempt pod eviction (pending other checks like PDB). Only positive values are allowed. Overrides global '--min-replicas' flag.

updateMode.description: Controls when autoscaler applies changes to the pod resources. The default is 'Auto'.

defaultVolumes.description:
Default volumes that are mounted into pods. In most cases, these should not be changed.
Use `extraVolumes`/`extraVolumeMounts` for additional custom volumes.

enabled.description: Creates a HorizontalPodAutoscaler for the deployment

extraArgs.description: Extra args for the Promtail container.

extraContainers.description:
Extra containers created as part of a Promtail Deployment resource
- spec for Container:
https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.23/#container-v1-core
Note that the key is used as the `name` field, i.e. below will create a
container named `promtail-proxy`.
promtail-proxy:
image: nginx
...

extraEnv.description:
Extra environment variables. Set up tracing enviroment variables here if .Values.config.enableTracing is true.
Tracing currently only support configure via environment variables. See:
https://grafana.com/docs/loki/latest/clients/promtail/configuration/#tracing_config
https://www.jaegertracing.io/docs/1.16/client-features/

extraEnvFrom.description: Extra environment variables from secrets or configmaps

extraObjects.description:
Extra K8s manifests to deploy
- apiVersion: "kubernetes-client.io/v1"
kind: ExternalSecret
metadata:
name: promtail-secrets
spec:
backendType: gcpSecretsManager
data:
- key: promtail-oauth2-creds
name: client_secret

extraPorts.description:
Configure additional ports and services. For each configured port, a corresponding service is created.
See values.yaml for details
syslog:
name: tcp-syslog
annotations: {}
labels: {}
containerPort: 1514
protocol: TCP
service:
type: ClusterIP
clusterIP: null
port: 1514
externalIPs: []
nodePort: null
loadBalancerIP: null
loadBalancerSourceRanges: []
externalTrafficPolicy: null
ingress:
For Kubernetes >= 1.18 you should specify the ingress-controller via the field ingressClassName
See https://kubernetes.io/blog/2020/04/02/improvements-to-the-ingress-api-in-kubernetes-1.18/#specifying-the-class-of-an-ingress
ingressClassName: nginx
Values can be templated
annotations: {}
kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx
kubernetes.io/tls-acme: "true"
paths: "/"
hosts:
- chart-example.local
tls: []
- secretName: chart-example-tls
hosts:
- chart-example.local

extraVolumeMounts.description: Extra volume mounts together. Corresponds to `extraVolumes`.

extraVolumes.description: Extra volumes to be added in addition to those specified under `defaultVolumes`.

imageRegistry.description: Allow parent charts to override registry hostname

hostNetwork.description: Controls whether the pod has the `hostNetwork` flag set.

httpPathPrefix.description: Base path to server all API routes fro

tag.description: Overrides the image tag whose default is the chart's appVersion

imagePullSecrets.description: Image pull secrets for Docker images

namespace.description:
The name of the Namespace to deploy
If not set, `.Release.Namespace` is used

port.description: Specify the k8s API endpoint port

namespaceSelector.description: Specifies the namespaces which are allowed to access the metrics port

podSelector.description:
Specifies the Pods which are allowed to access the metrics port.
As this is cross-namespace communication, you also neeed the namespaceSelector.

enabled.description: Specifies whether Network Policies should be created

cidrs.description: Specifies specific network CIDRs you want to limit access to

port.description: Specify the k8s API endpoint port

cidrs.description:
Specifies specific network CIDRs which are allowed to access the metrics port.
In case you use namespaceSelector, you also have to specify your kubelet networks here.
The metrics ports are also used for probes.

namespaceSelector.description: Specifies the namespaces which are allowed to access the metrics port

podSelector.description:
Specifies the Pods which are allowed to access the metrics port.
As this is cross-namespace communication, you also neeed the namespaceSelector.

create.description: Specifies whether RBAC resources are to be created

pspEnabled.description: Specifies whether a PodSecurityPolicy is to be created

resources.description:
Resource requests and limits
limits:
cpu: 200m
memory: 128Mi
requests:
cpu: 100m
memory: 128Mi

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Automatically mount a ServiceAccount's API credentials

imagePullSecrets.description: Image pull secrets for the service account

name.description:
The name of the ServiceAccount to use.
If not set and `create` is true, a name is generated using the fullname template

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Automatically mount a ServiceAccount's API credentials

create.description: Specifies whether a ServiceAccount should be created

imagePullSecrets.description: Image pull secrets for the service account

name.description:
The name of the ServiceAccount to use.
If not set and `create` is true, a name is generated using the fullname template

metricRelabelings.description:
ServiceMonitor relabel configs to apply to samples as the last
step before ingestion
https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/blob/master/Documentation/api.md#relabelconfig
(defines `metric_relabel_configs`)

items.description:
RelabelConfig allows dynamic rewriting of the label set for targets, alerts,
scraped samples and remote write samples.

More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

action.description:
action to perform based on the regex matching.

`Uppercase` and `Lowercase` actions require Prometheus >= v2.36.0.
`DropEqual` and `KeepEqual` actions require Prometheus >= v2.41.0.

Default: "Replace"

modulus.description:
modulus to take of the hash of the source label values.

Only applicable when the action is `HashMod`.

regex.description: regex defines the regular expression against which the extracted value is matched.

replacement.description:
replacement value against which a Replace action is performed if the
regular expression matches.

Regex capture groups are available.

separator.description: separator defines the string between concatenated SourceLabels.

sourceLabels.description:
sourceLabels defines the source labels select values from existing labels. Their content is
concatenated using the configured Separator and matched against the
configured regular expression.

items.description:
LabelName is a valid Prometheus label name.
For Prometheus 3.x, a label name is valid if it contains UTF-8 characters.
For Prometheus 2.x, a label name is only valid if it contains ASCII characters, letters, numbers, as well as underscores.

targetLabel.description:
targetLabel defines the label to which the resulting string is written in a replacement.

It is mandatory for `Replace`, `HashMod`, `Lowercase`, `Uppercase`,
`KeepEqual` and `DropEqual` actions.

Regex capture groups are available.

any.description:
any defines the boolean describing whether all namespaces are selected in contrast to a
list restricting them.

matchNames.description: matchNames defines the list of namespace names to select from.

relabelings.description:
ServiceMonitor relabel configs to apply to samples before scraping
https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/blob/master/Documentation/api.md#relabelconfig
(defines `relabel_configs`)

items.description:
RelabelConfig allows dynamic rewriting of the label set for targets, alerts,
scraped samples and remote write samples.

More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

action.description:
action to perform based on the regex matching.

`Uppercase` and `Lowercase` actions require Prometheus >= v2.36.0.
`DropEqual` and `KeepEqual` actions require Prometheus >= v2.41.0.

Default: "Replace"

modulus.description:
modulus to take of the hash of the source label values.

Only applicable when the action is `HashMod`.

regex.description: regex defines the regular expression against which the extracted value is matched.

replacement.description:
replacement value against which a Replace action is performed if the
regular expression matches.

Regex capture groups are available.

separator.description: separator defines the string between concatenated SourceLabels.

sourceLabels.description:
sourceLabels defines the source labels select values from existing labels. Their content is
concatenated using the configured Separator and matched against the
configured regular expression.

items.description:
LabelName is a valid Prometheus label name.
For Prometheus 3.x, a label name is valid if it contains UTF-8 characters.
For Prometheus 2.x, a label name is only valid if it contains ASCII characters, letters, numbers, as well as underscores.

targetLabel.description:
targetLabel defines the label to which the resulting string is written in a replacement.

It is mandatory for `Replace`, `HashMod`, `Lowercase`, `Uppercase`,
`KeepEqual` and `DropEqual` actions.

Regex capture groups are available.

anyOf.description: scheme defines the HTTP scheme to use when scraping the metrics.

scheme.description: ServiceMonitor will use http by default, but you can pick https as well

scrapeTimeout.description: ServiceMonitor scrape timeout in Go duration format (e.g. 15s)

targetLabels.description:
ServiceMonitor will add labels from the service to the Prometheus metric
https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/blob/main/Documentation/api.md#servicemonitorspec

anyOf.description: tlsConfig defines TLS configuration used by the client.

ca.description: ca defines the Certificate authority used when verifying server certificates.

configMap.description: configMap defines the ConfigMap containing data to use for the targets.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the ConfigMap or its key must be defined

secret.description: secret defines the Secret containing data to use for the targets.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

caFile.description: caFile defines the path to the CA cert in the Prometheus container to use for the targets.

cert.description: cert defines the Client certificate to present when doing client-authentication.

configMap.description: configMap defines the ConfigMap containing data to use for the targets.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the ConfigMap or its key must be defined

secret.description: secret defines the Secret containing data to use for the targets.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

certFile.description: certFile defines the path to the client cert file in the Prometheus container for the targets.

insecureSkipVerify.description: insecureSkipVerify defines how to disable target certificate validation.

keyFile.description: keyFile defines the path to the client key file in the Prometheus container for the targets.

keySecret.description: keySecret defines the Secret containing the client key file for the targets.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

maxVersion.description:
maxVersion defines the maximum acceptable TLS version.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.41.0 or Thanos >= v0.31.0.

minVersion.description:
minVersion defines the minimum acceptable TLS version.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.35.0 or Thanos >= v0.28.0.

serverName.description: serverName is used to verify the hostname for the targets.

tlsConfig.description: ServiceMonitor will use these tlsConfig settings to make the health check requests

prometheusRule.description: Prometheus rules will be deployed for alerting purposes

rules.description:
namespace:
- alert: PromtailRequestErrors
expr: 100 * sum(rate(promtail_request_duration_seconds_count{status_code=~"5..|failed"}[1m])) by (namespace, job, route, instance) / sum(rate(promtail_request_duration_seconds_count[1m])) by (namespace, job, route, instance) > 10
for: 5m
labels:
severity: critical
annotations:
description: |
The {{ $labels.job }} {{ $labels.route }} is experiencing
{{ printf \"%.2f\" $value }} errors.
VALUE = {{ $value }}
LABELS = {{ $labels }}
summary: Promtail request errors (instance {{ $labels.instance }})
- alert: PromtailRequestLatency
expr: histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(promtail_request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) by (le)) > 1
for: 5m
labels:
severity: critical
annotations:
summary: Promtail request latency (instance {{ $labels.instance }})
description: |
The {{ $labels.job }} {{ $labels.route }} is experiencing
{{ printf \"%.2f\" $value }}s 99th percentile latency.
VALUE = {{ $value }}
LABELS = {{ $labels }}

items.description:
Rule describes an alerting or recording rule
See Prometheus documentation: [alerting](https://www.prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/alerting_rules/) or [recording](https://www.prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/recording_rules/#recording-rules) rule

alert.description:
Name of the alert. Must be a valid label value.
Only one of `record` and `alert` must be set.

annotations.description:
Annotations to add to each alert.
Only valid for alerting rules.

for.description: Alerts are considered firing once they have been returned for this long.

keep_firing_for.description: KeepFiringFor defines how long an alert will continue firing after the condition that triggered it has cleared.

record.description:
Name of the time series to output to. Must be a valid metric name.
Only one of `record` and `alert` must be set.

enabled.description: If enabled, ServiceMonitor resources for Prometheus Operator are created

metricRelabelings.description:
ServiceMonitor relabel configs to apply to samples as the last
step before ingestion
https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/blob/master/Documentation/api.md#relabelconfig
(defines `metric_relabel_configs`)

Only applicable when the action is `HashMod`.

replacement.description:
replacement value against which a Replace action is performed if the
regular expression matches.

Regex capture groups are available.

sourceLabels.description:

concatenated using the configured Separator and matched against the
configured regular expression.

targetLabel.description:
targetLabel defines the label to which the resulting string is written in a replacement.

It is mandatory for `Replace`, `HashMod`, `Lowercase`, `Uppercase`,
`KeepEqual` and `DropEqual` actions.

Regex capture groups are available.

expr: 100 * sum(rate(promtail_request_duration_seconds_count{status_code=~"5..|failed"}[1m])) by (namespace, job, route, instance) / sum(rate(promtail_request_duration_seconds_count[1m])) by (namespace, job, route, instance) > 10
for: 5m
labels:
severity: critical
annotations:
description: |
The {{ $labels.job }} {{ $labels.route }} is experiencing
{{ printf \"%.2f\" $value }} errors.
VALUE = {{ $value }}
LABELS = {{ $labels }}
summary: Promtail request errors (instance {{ $labels.instance }})
- alert: PromtailRequestLatency
expr: histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(promtail_request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) by (le)) > 1
for: 5m
labels:
severity: critical
annotations:
summary: Promtail request latency (instance {{ $labels.instance }})
description: |
The {{ $labels.job }} {{ $labels.route }} is experiencing
{{ printf \"%.2f\" $value }}s 99th percentile latency.
VALUE = {{ $value }}
LABELS = {{ $labels }}

items.description:
Rule describes an alerting or recording rule
See Prometheus documentation: [alerting](https://www.prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/alerting_rules/) or [recording](https://www.prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/recording_rules/#recording-rules) rule

alert.description:
Name of the alert. Must be a valid label value.
Only one of `record` and `alert` must be set.

annotations.description:
Annotations to add to each alert.
Only valid for alerting rules.

for.description: Alerts are considered firing once they have been returned for this long.

keep_firing_for.description: KeepFiringFor defines how long an alert will continue firing after the condition that triggered it has cleared.

record.description:
Name of the time series to output to. Must be a valid metric name.
Only one of `record` and `alert` must be set.

relabelings.description:
ServiceMonitor relabel configs to apply to samples before scraping
https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/blob/master/Documentation/api.md#relabelconfig
(defines `relabel_configs`)

items.description:
RelabelConfig allows dynamic rewriting of the label set for targets, alerts,
scraped samples and remote write samples.

More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

action.description:
action to perform based on the regex matching.

`Uppercase` and `Lowercase` actions require Prometheus >= v2.36.0.
`DropEqual` and `KeepEqual` actions require Prometheus >= v2.41.0.

Default: "Replace"

modulus.description:
modulus to take of the hash of the source label values.

Only applicable when the action is `HashMod`.

regex.description: regex defines the regular expression against which the extracted value is matched.

replacement.description:
replacement value against which a Replace action is performed if the
regular expression matches.

Regex capture groups are available.

separator.description: separator defines the string between concatenated SourceLabels.

sourceLabels.description:
sourceLabels defines the source labels select values from existing labels. Their content is
concatenated using the configured Separator and matched against the
configured regular expression.

items.description:
LabelName is a valid Prometheus label name.
For Prometheus 3.x, a label name is valid if it contains UTF-8 characters.
For Prometheus 2.x, a label name is only valid if it contains ASCII characters, letters, numbers, as well as underscores.

targetLabel.description:
targetLabel defines the label to which the resulting string is written in a replacement.

It is mandatory for `Replace`, `HashMod`, `Lowercase`, `Uppercase`,
`KeepEqual` and `DropEqual` actions.

Regex capture groups are available.

scheme.description: ServiceMonitor will use http by default, but you can pick https as well

scrapeTimeout.description: ServiceMonitor scrape timeout in Go duration format (e.g. 15s)

targetLabels.description:
ServiceMonitor will add labels from the service to the Prometheus metric
https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/blob/main/Documentation/api.md#servicemonitorspec

tlsConfig.description: ServiceMonitor will use these tlsConfig settings to make the health check requests

config.description:
limits:
cpu: 200m
memory: 128Mi
requests:
cpu: 100m
memory: 128Mi

serverPort.description: The port of the config-reloader server

containerSecurityContext.description: The security context for containers for sidecar config-reloader

extraArgs.description: Extra args for the config-reloader container.

extraEnv.description: Extra environment variables for sidecar config-reloader

extraEnvFrom.description: Extra environment variables from secrets or configmaps for sidecar config-reloader

pullPolicy.description: Docker image pull policy for sidecar config-reloader

registry.description: The Docker registry for sidecar config-reloader

repository.description: Docker image repository for sidecar config-reloader

tag.description: Docker image tag for sidecar config-reloader

resources.description: Resource requests and limits for sidecar config-reloader

tolerations.description: Tolerations for pods. By default, pods will be scheduled on master/control-plane nodes.

updateStrategy.description: The update strategy for the DaemonSet
